unit_checks.behavior: returns a soft-failure response when event.detail.ddbId is missing
loads the record by ddbId, validates merged JSON, and returns success when validation passes
returns validation errors when the validator reports failure
marks the record as errored through shared error handling when the record is missing
converts unexpected exceptions into a failure response containing the exception message
updates failure status on the source record and preserves error context
publishes the validated payload with FIFO-compatible grouping and deduplication fields preserved

integration_checks.service: Cosmos DB or Table Storage repository replacement
integration_checks.verify: the handler reads the source record by id using the Azure SDK client path and writes validation status updates through the same owning repository
the handler publishes the validated payload to a queue/topic using the Azure SDK, with FIFO-equivalent session/group semantics when required
the function entrypoint receives the migrated event payload and routes to handler logic without importing AWS SDKs
all Azure client constructors and network calls are mockable in tests and no live Azure calls are performed

architecture_checks.check: function_app.py exists under the validation module and exposes the Azure Function entrypoint
tests directory exists with unit tests covering handler, error handling, and queue publish behavior
infrastructure/validation/main.bicep exists and declares the Function App, required Storage Account, Application Insights, Managed Identity, and any needed backing services
no AWS imports remain in production code, including boto3, botocore, or aws_clients references
all Azure SDK clients are lazily constructed and mockable without module-scope network calls or environment lookups
code coverage for the module remains at or above 80%
module remains self-contained and does not import sibling packages outside the validation module
